RYE BREAD WITH CARAWAY + RYE BREAD WITH SUNFLOWER SEEDS

RECIPE 1: RYE BREAD WITH CARAWAY

Ingredients:
- 2 cups rye flour
- 1 cup whole wheat flour
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
- 2 tablespoons caraway seeds
- 2 tablespoons honey
- 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
- 1 1/2 teaspoons instant yeast
- 1 1/4 cups warm water

Instructions for Caraway Bread:
Mix rye flour, whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, salt, and caraway seeds.
Add yeast to warm water with honey and oil.
Combine wet and dry ingredients, stir until shaggy dough forms.
Knead 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place in greased bowl, cover, let rise 90 minutes.
Shape into round loaf, place on parchment paper.
Cover and let proof 60 minutes.
Score top with sharp knife in decorative pattern.
Bake at 450°F for 35-40 minutes until golden.
Cool on wire rack.

---

RECIPE 2: RYE BREAD WITH SUNFLOWER SEEDS

Ingredients:
- 2 cups rye flour
- 1 cup whole wheat flour
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
- 1 cup sunflower seeds
- 1/4 cup raw sunflower seeds for topping
- 2 tablespoons honey
- 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
- 1 1/2 teaspoons instant yeast
- 1 1/4 cups warm water

Instructions for Sunflower Seed Bread:
Combine rye flour, whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, salt, and sunflower seeds.
Dissolve yeast in warm water with honey and oil.
Mix wet and dry ingredients until dough forms.
Knead 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place in oiled bowl, cover, let rise 90 minutes until doubled.
Shape into round loaf on parchment paper.
Brush top lightly with water.
Press additional sunflower seeds onto top surface.
Cover and let proof 60 minutes.
Score top with knife.
Bake at 450°F for 35-40 minutes until crust is dark brown.
Cool completely on wire rack before slicing.

Both breads:
Prep time: 20 minutes
Rising time: 150 minutes
Baking time: 40 minutes
Each makes: 1 loaf

Storage: Keep in paper bag at room temperature up to 3 days.
Freeze wrapped loaves up to 3 months.
